engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Traviq Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Traviq in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. Note: Traviq uses GM/Opel inline-4 engines, not Subaru boxer units.

cards.altText: Subaru Traviq Z18XE engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade from 80k miles, causing rattling noise and potential chain skip.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup restricts exhaust gas recirculation, triggering warning lights and rough idle.

cards.altText: Subaru Traviq Z22SE engine - petrol 2.2L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption via valve stem seals - worn seals allow oil into combustion chambers, causing blue smoke on startup.
Coolant leak at thermostat housing - plastic housing cracks with age, leading to overheating if unchecked.

cards.altText: Subaru Traviq X18XE1 engine - petrol 1.8L replacement UK
knownFailures: Intake manifold swirl flap failure - plastic actuator arms break, causing reduced power and fault codes.
Camshaft position sensor wear - intermittent starting issues and rough running after 70k miles.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £895-£1,750 (supply only) Estimated from comparable Z18XE units - actual quotes may vary.

engineGuide.h2: Subaru Traviq Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Subaru Traviq across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: The Z18XE powered most Traviq imports, offering reliable 116hp performance with simple SOHC design and widespread GM parts availability across UK breakers.
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - plastic guides degrade from 80k miles, causing rattling noise.
EGR valve clogging - carbon buildup triggers warning lights and rough idle on high-mileage units.
Intake swirl flap failure - plastic actuator arms break, reducing power and triggering fault codes.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z18XE Subaru Traviq engine replacement

entries.title: 1.8L Petrol
entries.history: Early-production Z18XE variant with minor calibration differences, found on first-year Traviq imports before mid-cycle updates.
commonFailures: Camshaft position sensor wear - intermittent starting issues after 70k miles on early units.
Coolant thermostat housing cracks - plastic housing fails with age, risking overheating.
entries.cta: Get quotes for X18XE1 Subaru Traviq engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Petrol
entries.history: The larger-displacement option for Traviq, delivering 147-150hp with stronger torque for loaded MPV use, common on higher-spec imports.
commonFailures: Oil consumption via valve stem seals - worn seals cause blue smoke on startup after 90k miles.
Thermostat housing coolant leaks - plastic housing cracks, leading to overheating if unchecked.
Timing chain guide wear - nylon guides degrade, causing chain rattle on high-mileage examples.
entries.cta: Get quotes for Z22SE Subaru Traviq engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Petrol
entries.history: Late-production Z22SE variant with updated ECU mapping for improved emissions compliance on final-year Traviq imports.
commonFailures: EGR cooler carbon buildup - restricted flow triggers warning lights on short-journey vehicles.
Oil filter housing gasket leaks - rubber seals harden with age, causing external oil seepage.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Traviq Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Traviq's GM-sourced Z18XE and Z22SE engines are generally robust but develop age-related issues common to early-2000s GM Family 1/Family II units. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. This section helps you decide between repair and replacement based on your Traviq's symptoms and value.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Z18XE/Z22SE Engines Develop Rattles
problems.rootCause: Plastic timing chain guides and tensioner pistons degrade with heat and mileage, allowing chain slack that causes rattling and potential valve timing issues.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, and chain if stretched; reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: 40-60k miles if oil changes maintained; best before symptoms appear

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Timing Kit + Oil Pump
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete timing kit, oil pump drive, water pump, seals; full retime
repairOptions.longevity: 60-80k miles; recommended if engine has rattled but not jumped time

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z18XE/Z22SE with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; eliminates all internal wear concerns; best for high-mileage Traviqs

problems.recommendation: If your Traviq has under 100k miles and the chain hasn't jumped time, a preventative timing kit replacement at a specialist offers excellent value. For engines over 120k miles or with confirmed timing jump, a rebuilt engine is often more economical than extensive repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Z18XE/Z22SE engine prices for your Subaru Traviq

problems.group: Oil Consumption via Valve Stem Seals
problems.h4: Oil Consumption via Valve Stem Seals - Z22SE Blue Smoke Issue
problems.rootCause: Rubber valve stem seals harden and shrink with age/heat, allowing oil to seep into combustion chambers, especially after cold starts or extended idling.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace seals using air pressure method without head removal; new PCV valve
repairOptions.longevity: 30-50k miles; cost-effective if compression is good and no other wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove head, replace seals, valves, guides; skim head if needed; full gasket set
repairOptions.longevity: 50-70k miles; recommended if valves show wear or compression is marginal

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z22SE with updated seals, pistons, rings; bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; eliminates all top-end wear; best for high-mileage or heavily used Traviqs

problems.recommendation: For Traviqs with good compression and no other issues, in-situ seal replacement is a sensible first step. If the engine has high mileage or other wear signs, a rebuilt unit offers better long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Z22SE Subaru Traviq engine replacement with upgraded seals

problems.h4: EGR Valve & Cooler Clogging - Warning Lights and Rough Idle
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Traviq all variants (2001-2005, Z18XE, Z22SE)
problems.rootCause: Exhaust gas recirculation passages accumulate carbon deposits from short journeys and infrequent servicing, restricting flow and triggering fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove and clean EGR valve/cooler or replace unit; clear fault codes; road test
repairOptions.longevity: 20-40k miles if driving patterns improve; suitable for early-stage clogging

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Full Intake Clean + Software Update
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Clean intake manifold, throttle body, EGR passages; update ECU map if available
repairOptions.longevity: 40-60k miles; recommended for vehicles used primarily on short trips

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with clean EGR system, updated components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; eliminates all carbon-related issues; best for high-mileage or neglected units

problems.recommendation: Start with an EGR clean if fault codes are recent and performance loss is mild. For recurring issues or heavily carboned engines, a rebuilt unit with clean internals offers better reliability for Traviq owners planning long-term use.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Z18XE/Z22SE engine prices for your Subaru Traviq

problems.h4: Coolant Leak at Thermostat Housing - Overheating Risk
problems.affectedModels: Subaru Traviq all variants (2001-2005, Z18XE, Z22SE)
problems.rootCause: Plastic thermostat housing becomes brittle with age and heat cycles, developing cracks that allow coolant to escape, risking engine overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace plastic housing with updated metal/aluminium unit; new gasket, coolant refill
repairOptions.longevity: 40-60k miles; cost-effective fix if no overheating damage occurred

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace housing, thermostat, water pump, hoses; pressure test system
repairOptions.longevity: 60-80k miles; recommended if hoses are original or pump shows wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new cooling components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; essential if overheating caused head warpage or gasket failure

problems.recommendation: If caught early with no overheating damage, housing replacement is straightforward and affordable. If the engine overheated significantly, a rebuilt unit is often safer than risking hidden head or gasket damage.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Z18XE/Z22SE Subaru Traviq engine replacement with cooling system check

problems.h4: Intake Swirl Flap Failure - Reduced Power and Fault Codes
problems.rootCause: Plastic swirl flap actuator arms inside the intake manifold become brittle and snap, causing flaps to stick and triggering power loss and fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Install blanking plates or repair kit to secure flaps; clear fault codes; road test
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if flaps are intact; suitable for early-stage actuator wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace entire intake manifold with updated unit; new gaskets, sensors transferred
repairOptions.longevity: 60-80k miles; recommended if flaps are damaged or manifold is heavily carboned

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt Z18XE with clean intake system, updated components, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; eliminates all intake-related issues; best for high-mileage or neglected units

problems.recommendation: For early swirl flap issues, a blanking kit is a proven, permanent fix. If the manifold is heavily carboned or flaps are damaged, manifold replacement or a rebuilt engine offers better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt Z18XE engine prices for your Subaru Traviq

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Traviq
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru Traviq's GM-sourced engines are robust when maintained, but age-related failures can be costly to repair. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand these unique Japanese-import MPVs. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing common failure points, plus a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and keep your Traviq on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Traviq Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Traviq and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Subaru Traviq Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Traviq's production timeline (2001-2005), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: First-year Traviq imports featured early-spec Z18XE and Z22SE engines with pre-facelift calibration and components.
keyChanges: Subaru Traviq launched in Japan as rebadged Opel Zafira A
Initial engines: Z18XE (1.8L) and Z22SE (2.2L) with early ECU mapping
Trim levels: S, M, L with manual or 4-speed automatic transmission
Front-wheel drive only; no AWD option on any Traviq variant
knownFor: Early Z18XE units prone to timing chain tensioner wear from factory
X18XE1 camshaft position sensors show higher failure rates than later Z18XE
Z22SE valve stem seals begin hardening after 80k miles on early units
Plastic thermostat housing cracks common on high-mileage examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2001-2003 Subaru Traviq replacement engines
years.ticker: Traviq Z18XE timing chain rattle • Traviq Z22SE blue smoke cold start • Traviq thermostat housing leak
enquiries: Traviq Z22SE blue smoke cold start

years.description: Final-year Traviq imports featured updated Z18XE and Z22SE engines with revised ECU mapping for improved emissions compliance.
keyChanges: Mid-cycle update with revised ECU calibration for Euro 4 emissions
Introduction of Z22YH variant with updated mapping for Elite trim
Trim expansion: SE and Elite variants added with enhanced equipment
Minor component updates to timing guides and thermostat housing material
knownFor: Updated Z18XE shows improved timing component longevity vs early units
Z22YH ECU mapping reduces EGR-related fault codes on short journeys
Late-production thermostat housings less prone to cracking but still monitor
Overall reliability improved but age-related wear remains primary concern

faq.h2: Subaru Traviq Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Traviq engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Traviq engine replacement typically costs £895-£2,450 for a rebuilt unit (supply only), depending on engine code (Z18XE vs Z22SE) and supplier. Supply & fit adds £495-£895 labour.
keyPoints: Z18XE 1.8L rebuilt engines: £895-£1,850 supply only
Z22SE 2.2L rebuilt engines: £1,095-£2,150 supply only
Professional fitting typically adds £495-£895 depending on location
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
items.cta: Compare Subaru Traviq engine replacement quotes now

items.question: What engine codes fit the Subaru Traviq?
items.answer: The Subaru Traviq uses GM-sourced Z18XE (1.8L) or Z22SE/Z22YH (2.2L) inline-4 petrol engines, not Subaru boxer engines. Verify your exact code via registration lookup.
keyPoints: Z18XE: 1.8L SOHC, 116-125hp, most common on S/M/L trims
Z22SE/Z22YH: 2.2L SOHC, 147-150hp, higher-spec variants
X18XE1: early 1.8L variant, pre-facelift models only
All are GM Family 1/Family II inline-4 units, front-wheel drive only
items.cta: Check your Subaru Traviq engine code compatibility instantly

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru boxer engine in my Traviq?
items.answer: No, the Subaru Traviq was built on the GM/Opel Zafira platform and uses GM inline-4 engines. Subaru boxer engines are not compatible without extensive, costly modification.
keyPoints: Traviq uses GM Family 1/Family II inline-4 engines, not Subaru boxer architecture
Engine mounts, wiring harness, ECU and drivetrain are GM/Opel specific
Boxer engine swap would require custom fabrication and is not cost-effective
Stick with Z18XE or Z22SE replacements for correct fitment and reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for correct Z18XE/Z22SE Subaru Traviq engine replacement

items.question: How long does a reconditioned Subaru Traviq engine last?
items.answer: A properly reconditioned Z18XE or Z22SE engine, with regular servicing, can last 80,000-120,000+ miles. Key is using quality parts and maintaining oil change intervals.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines include upgraded timing components and seals to address common failures
Regular oil changes (every 10k miles max) critical for longevity
Timing chain kit replacement recommended at 80k miles preventative
12-month unlimited mileage warranty provides initial peace of mind
items.cta: View Subaru Traviq reconditioned engine warranty terms

items.question: Does a Subaru Traviq engine replacement include warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all rebuilt and reconditioned Subaru Traviq engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier; confirm before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Some suppliers offer 24-month options for premium peace of mind
Warranty typically requires professional fitting; confirm supplier terms
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare Subaru Traviq engine warranty options from UK specialists

items.question: How long does it take to replace a Subaru Traviq engine?
items.answer: Professional Subaru Traviq engine replacement typically takes 1-2 days for supply & fit, depending on engine code, transmission type and specialist availability.
keyPoints: Z18XE 1.8L replacement: typically 1 day for experienced GM/Opel specialists
Z22SE 2.2L replacement: 1-2 days due to slightly larger size and ancillaries
Automatic transmission variants may add 2-4 hours for ECU adaptation
Next-day engine dispatch available from many UK suppliers for urgent needs
items.cta: Book Subaru Traviq engine supply & fit with UK specialists

items.question: Do I need to reprogram the ECU after a Subaru Traviq engine replacement?
items.answer: Usually not for like-for-like Z18XE or Z22SE replacements, but ECU adaptation may be needed for optimal idle and emissions. Confirm with your specialist before fitting.
keyPoints: Like-for-like engine code swaps typically plug-and-play for basic operation
ECU adaptation/reset recommended for optimal idle and emissions compliance
Some suppliers include basic ECU reset in supply & fit service
Diagnostic check post-fit ensures no fault codes and correct operation
items.cta: Find Subaru Traviq engine specialists with ECU adaptation capability

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used Traviq engines?
items.answer: Used engines are second-hand units with unknown history; reconditioned engines are cleaned, inspected and have worn parts replaced; rebuilt engines receive comprehensive overhaul with upgraded components.
keyPoints: Used: lowest cost, variable condition, shorter warranty (3-6 months typical)
Reconditioned: mid-price, worn parts replaced, 12-month warranty standard
Rebuilt: premium option, comprehensive overhaul with upgrades, 12-24 month warranty
Choose based on your Traviq's value, intended use and budget priorities
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru Traviq engine options

items.question: Can I drive my Subaru Traviq with a faulty engine to your fitting centre?
items.answer: If your Traviq's engine is knocking, smoking heavily, or has lost compression, do not drive it. Arrange recovery to avoid further damage; many specialists offer collection services.
keyPoints: Driving with severe engine damage risks catastrophic failure and higher repair costs
Minor issues like warning lights or mild oil consumption may allow careful driving
Many EngineMarket specialists offer vehicle collection for engine replacement
Confirm recovery options when requesting your engine replacement quote
items.cta: Get Subaru Traviq engine replacement quote with recovery options

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Subaru Traviq?
items.answer: Yes, if your Traviq is otherwise in good condition and has sentimental or practical value. A rebuilt engine (£1,095-£2,450) is often more economical than buying a replacement vehicle.
keyPoints: Traviq values are modest; engine replacement can extend life cost-effectively
Rebuilt engines address known failure points for improved long-term reliability
Consider overall vehicle condition: bodywork, interior, suspension before deciding
Compare total replacement cost vs. purchasing another used MPV of similar age
items.cta: Calculate if Subaru Traviq engine replacement is right for you
